reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Code reading verified: reconstruction.go:79 iterates Go map (sidecarByIndex) which has randomized iteration order, building cellsIndices without sorting before passing to RecoverCellsAndKZGProofs (line 86). Spec SG-024 explicitly requires 'assert cell_indices == sorted(cell_indices)' as invariant. Test at fulu__kzg__recover_cells_and_kzg_proofs_test.go:45-57 checks for sorted order and expects failure for unsorted, but uses early return pattern rather than calling the function, making library enforcement uncertain. Reed-Solomon recovery is mathematically order-dependent, so unsorted indices could produce incorrect reconstruction. However, cannot definitively confirm c-kzg-4844 v2.1.1 validates sorting at runtime vs expecting caller compliance. Downgraded from Medium to Low: single-client bug affecting Prysm (31% CL share), below the 33% threshold for Medium severity per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE severity_classification. Impact limited to incorrect PeerDAS reconstruction in specific scenarios.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Fulu Polynomial Commitments Sampling SG-024 (recover_cells_and_kzg_proofs): INV line 21-22 requires cell indices unique and in ascending order
